chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P. No.D-519 of 2026 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE For order on office objections. For hearing of MA No.2067/2026. For hearing of main case. 09.04.2026 Mr. Zahid Ali Khoso advocate for petitioners along with petitioners. Mr. Muhammad Ismail Bhutto, Additional A.G. Sindh along with Inspector Nizamuddin Shaikh SHO PS Oderolal on behalf of SSP Matiari and SIP Muhammad Arshad PS A-Section Nawabshah, District Shaheed Benazirabad. Mr. Dhanraj Palani advocate files Vakalatnama on behalf of respondents No.7 & 8, taken on record. The petitioners are present along with their learned counsel. Petitioner No.1 was Hindu and she has embraced Islam. Her new name is Mst. Zuhran, who submits that she being sui juris has contracted a lawful marriage with petitioner No.2, Gul Muhammad of her own free will and consent without any coercion or compulsion. It is further contended that respondent No.7, the father of petitioner No.1, being aggrieved by the said marriage, has lodged FIR No.17 of 2026 at Police Station Odero Lal Station, District Matiari, under Sections 365-B and 34 PPC and Section 3 of TIP Act, 2018. Today, the Investigating Officer is present and states that he has recorded the statement of petitioner No.1 under section 161 Cr.P.C., wherein she has reiterated the aforesaid facts and has categorically denied the allegations levelled in the FIR regarding kidnapping or wrongful confinement. In view of the statement of the alleged abductee, the Investigating Officer submits that he shall submit a report under Section 173 Cr.P.C. before the learned concerned Magistrate within seven days, for seeking appropriate orders in accordance with law.

Today, the Investigating Officer is present and states that he has recorded the statement of petitioner No.1 under section 161 Cr.P.C., wherein she has reiterated the aforesaid facts and has categorically denied the allegations levelled in the FIR regarding kidnapping or wrongful confinement. In view of the statement of the alleged abductee, the Investigating Officer submits that he shall submit a report under Section 173 Cr.P.C. before the learned concerned Magistrate within seven days, for seeking appropriate orders in accordance with law. At this juncture, learned counsel for respondents No.7 and 8 submits that petitioner No.1 is of minor age. He is at liberty to agitate such fact before the learned Magistrate while passing order over report under section 173 Cr.P.C. Accordingly, the instant petition stands disposed of in the above terms, along with the listed application. JUDGE JUDGE *Abdullah Channa/PS*
